en.extract:
The winding cisticola (Cisticola marginatus) is a species of bird in the family Cisticolidae. It has a scattered distribution across Africa south of the Sahara, and north of 11°S.


Taxonomy
The winding cisticola has several subspecies:

C. m. amphilectus Reichenow, 1875 Mauritania and Senegal to Ghana, south-western Cameroon and north-western Angola
C. m. zalingei Lynes, 1930 northern Nigeria to western Sudan
C. m. marginatus (Heuglin, 1869) southern Sudan and northern Uganda
C. m. nyansae Neumann, 1905 central DRCongo to Uganda and Kenya
C. m. suahelicus Neumann, 1905 south-eastern DRCongo, Tanzania and north-eastern Zambia
The Clements list (2017) and the Howard and Moore list (2014) lump the rufous-winged cisticola, Luapula cisticola, coastal cisticola and Ethiopian cisticola with the above subspecies as the winding cisticola Cisticola galactotes (sensu lato). The Clements list (2017) recognizes 11 subspecies.


Habitat
Its natural habitats are tropical seasonally wet or flooded lowland grassland and swamps.


Gallery


References

The '''winding cisticola''' ('''''Cisticola marginatus''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Cisticolidae]]. It has a scattered distribution across Africa south of the [[Sahara]], and north of 11°S.

==Taxonomy==
The winding cisticola has several [[subspecies]]:<ref name="Gill&Donsker2017">{{cite web|last1=Gill|first1=F.|last2=Donsker|first2=D.|title=IOC World Bird List |url=http://www.worldbirdnames.org/new/bow/grassbirds/|accessdate=30 October 2020 |publisher=International Ornithological Union}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|last1=Ryan|first1=Peter|editor1-last=del Hoyo|editor1-first=J.|editor2-last=Elliott|editor2-first=A.|editor3-last=Sargatal|editor3-first=J.|editor4-last=Christie|editor4-first=D.A.|editor5-last=de Juana|editor5-first=E.|title=Handbook of the Birds of the World Alive|date=2017|publisher=Lynx Edicions|location=Barcelona|url=http://www.hbw.com/node/58622|accessdate=21 August 2017|language=en|chapter=Winding Cisticola (Cisticola marginatus)}}</ref><ref name=avibase>{{avibase|1DA06308|Cisticola marginatus}}</ref>
* ''C. m. amphilectus'' <small>Reichenow, 1875</small> Mauritania and Senegal to Ghana, south-western Cameroon and north-western Angola
* ''C. m. zalingei'' <small>Lynes, 1930</small> northern Nigeria to western Sudan
* ''C. m. marginatus'' <small>(Heuglin, 1869)</small> southern Sudan and northern Uganda
* ''C. m. nyansae'' <small>Neumann, 1905</small> central DRCongo to Uganda and Kenya
* ''C. m. suahelicus'' <small>Neumann, 1905</small> south-eastern DRCongo, Tanzania and north-eastern Zambia

The [[The Clements Checklist of Birds of the World|Clements]] list (2017) and the [[Howard and Moore Complete Checklist of the Birds of the World|Howard and Moore]] list (2014) lump the [[rufous-winged cisticola]], [[Luapula cisticola]], [[coastal cisticola]] and [[Ethiopian cisticola]] with the above subspecies as the '''winding cisticola ''Cisticola galactotes'' (sensu lato)'''.<ref name=avibase/> The Clements list (2017) recognizes 11 subspecies.<ref>{{cite web|title=Clements Checklist|url=https://www.birds.cornell.edu/clementschecklist/|website=www.birds.cornell.edu|accessdate=21 August 2017|language=en}}</ref>
==Habitat==

Its natural [[habitat]]s are tropical seasonally wet or flooded lowland [[grassland]] and [[swamp]]s.
